Simulation Analysis Report

Comparing Conservative, Realistic, and Optimistic Effect Scenarios

Author

Simulation Study

Published

February 10, 2026

Introduction

This report presents a comprehensive analysis of simulation results across three effect scenarios:

  • Conservative: Smaller effect sizes with stricter criteria
  • Realistic: Moderate effect sizes with balanced criteria
  • Optimistic: Larger effect sizes with more lenient criteria

The analysis examines effect sizes across three time windows (0-10s, 10-20s, 20-60s) and hypothesis support rates.

Data Loading and Preparation

Show code 
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)

# Ensure proper relative path if rendering from reports/qmd/
# If rendering from project root, adjusted path logic is needed.
# Here we assume the knitting root is set to the project directory (common stricture)
# or we dynamically find the scripts folder.

# Source the centralized data loader
# Note: Adjust path depending on where you render from. 
# Assuming project root context for now.
source("../../scripts/load_results.R")

# Load simulation results
all_data <- load_simulation_results(data_dir = "../../data/csv")

Summary Statistics

Overall Sample Sizes

Show code 
sample_summary <- all_data %>%
  group_by(scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    N_Simulations = n(),
    Mean_Participants = mean(n_participants),
    Range_Participants = paste(min(n_participants), "-", max(n_participants))
  )

kable(sample_summary, digits = 1, caption = "Sample Characteristics by Scenario")
Sample Characteristics by Scenario
scenario N_Simulations Mean_Participants Range_Participants
Conservative 51 60.6 60 - 90
Realistic 54 62.2 60 - 90
Optimistic 57 64.7 60 - 120

Effect Sizes by Time Window

Show code 
effect_summary <- all_data %>%
  group_by(scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    Effect_0_10s_Mean = mean(mean_0_10s, na.rm = TRUE),
    Effect_0_10s_SD = sd(mean_0_10s, na.rm = TRUE),
    Effect_10_20s_Mean = mean(mean_10_20s, na.rm = TRUE),
    Effect_10_20s_SD = sd(mean_10_20s, na.rm = TRUE),
    Effect_20_60s_Mean = mean(mean_20_60s, na.rm = TRUE),
    Effect_20_60s_SD = sd(mean_20_60s, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

kable(effect_summary, digits = 4, 
      caption = "Mean Effect Sizes ± SD by Time Window")
Mean Effect Sizes ± SD by Time Window
scenario Effect_0_10s_Mean Effect_0_10s_SD Effect_10_20s_Mean Effect_10_20s_SD Effect_20_60s_Mean Effect_20_60s_SD
Conservative 0.2398 0.0285 0.1194 0.0278 0.0017 0.0227
Realistic 0.3479 0.0269 0.1537 0.0224 -0.0034 0.0286
Optimistic 0.4928 0.0186 0.2478 0.0225 0.0131 0.0296

Hypothesis Support Rates

Show code 
h2_summary <- all_data %>%
  group_by(scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    H2_Support_Rate_Pct = mean(h2_supported, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    N_Supported = sum(h2_supported, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

kable(h2_summary, digits = 2, 
      caption = "H2 Hypothesis Support Rates")
H2 Hypothesis Support Rates
scenario H2_Support_Rate_Pct N_Supported
Conservative 98.04 50
Realistic 92.59 50
Optimistic 87.72 50

Visualizations

Distribution Comparison: 0-10s Time Window

The 0-10s window shows the most pronounced differences across scenarios.

Show code 
ggplot(all_data, aes(x = mean_0_10s, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Conservative" = "#1f77b4", 
                               "Realistic" = "#ff7f0e", 
                               "Optimistic" = "#2ca02c")) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Effects: 0-10s Window",
       x = "Mean Effect Size",
       y = "Density",
       fill = "Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top", text = element_text(size = 12))

Distribution Comparison: 10-20s Time Window

The 10-20s window shows moderate effects, declining with time.

Show code 
ggplot(all_data, aes(x = mean_10_20s, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Conservative" = "#1f77b4", 
                               "Realistic" = "#ff7f0e", 
                               "Optimistic" = "#2ca02c")) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Effects: 10-20s Window",
       x = "Mean Effect Size",
       y = "Density",
       fill = "Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top", text = element_text(size = 12))

Distribution Comparison: 20-60s Time Window

The 20-60s window shows minimal and unstable effects near zero.

Show code 
ggplot(all_data, aes(x = mean_20_60s, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Conservative" = "#1f77b4", 
                               "Realistic" = "#ff7f0e", 
                               "Optimistic" = "#2ca02c")) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Effects: 20-60s Window",
       x = "Mean Effect Size",
       y = "Density",
       fill = "Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top", text = element_text(size = 12))

Boxplot Comparison Across Scenarios

Show code 
# Prepare data for boxplot
plot_data <- all_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean_0_10s, mean_10_20s, mean_20_60s),
               names_to = "time_window",
               values_to = "effect_size") %>%
  mutate(time_window = case_when(
    time_window == "mean_0_10s" ~ "0-10s",
    time_window == "mean_10_20s" ~ "10-20s",
    time_window == "mean_20_60s" ~ "20-60s"
  ))

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = scenario, y = effect_size, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~time_window, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Conservative" = "#1f77b4", 
                               "Realistic" = "#ff7f0e", 
                               "Optimistic" = "#2ca02c")) +
  labs(title = "Effect Size Distributions Across Time Windows",
       x = "Scenario",
       y = "Effect Size",
       fill = "Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        text = element_text(size = 12))

Mean Effects with Confidence Intervals

Show code 
# Calculate means and CIs for each scenario and time window
ci_data <- all_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean_0_10s, mean_10_20s, mean_20_60s),
               names_to = "Time_Window",
               values_to = "Effect_Size") %>%
  mutate(Time_Window = case_when(
    Time_Window == "mean_0_10s" ~ "0-10s",
    Time_Window == "mean_10_20s" ~ "10-20s",
    Time_Window == "mean_20_60s" ~ "20-60s"
  )) %>%
  group_by(scenario, Time_Window) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Effect_Size, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(Effect_Size, na.rm = TRUE),
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    SE = SD / sqrt(N),
    CI_lower = Mean - 1.96 * SE,
    CI_upper = Mean + 1.96 * SE
  )

ggplot(ci_data, aes(x = Time_Window, y = Mean, color = scenario)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_lower, ymax = CI_upper), 
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Conservative" = "#1f77b4", 
                                "Realistic" = "#ff7f0e", 
                                "Optimistic" = "#2ca02c")) +
  labs(title = "Mean Effect Sizes with 95% Confidence Intervals",
       x = "Time Window",
       y = "Mean Effect Size",
       color = "Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top", text = element_text(size = 12))

Hypothesis Support Rates

Show code 
h2_plot_data <- all_data %>%
  group_by(scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    Support_Rate = mean(h2_supported, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )

ggplot(h2_plot_data, aes(x = scenario, y = Support_Rate, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Conservative" = "#1f77b4", 
                               "Realistic" = "#ff7f0e", 
                               "Optimistic" = "#2ca02c")) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Support_Rate, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
  ylim(0, 105) +
  labs(title = "H2 Hypothesis Support Rates by Scenario",
       x = "Scenario",
       y = "Support Rate (%)",
       fill = "Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", text = element_text(size = 12))

Key Findings

Effect Size Patterns

  1. 0-10s Window: Shows strong, consistent effects across all scenarios
    • Conservative: 0.240 ± 0.028
    • Realistic: 0.348 ± 0.027
    • Optimistic: 0.493 ± 0.019
  2. 10-20s Window: Shows smaller but still substantial effects
    • Conservative: 0.119 ± 0.028
    • Realistic: 0.154 ± 0.022
    • Optimistic: 0.248 ± 0.023
  3. 20-60s Window: Effects diminish to near-zero
    • All scenarios: Effects cluster around 0 (range: -0.003 to +0.013)

Hypothesis Support

  • Conservative scenario: Highest support rate (98.04%)
  • Realistic scenario: Moderate support rate (92.59%)
  • Optimistic scenario: Lower support rate (87.72%)

The inverse relationship between effect size assumptions and support rates suggests that more stringent criteria in conservative scenarios lead to more consistent hypothesis support.

Conclusions

  1. Temporal Decay: Effects show clear temporal decay across the three time windows
  2. Scenario Sensitivity: Effect size assumptions significantly impact observed results
  3. Reliability: Conservative assumptions provide most reliable hypothesis confirmation
  4. Time Window Importance: The 0-10s window carries the most informative signal
